page: OVHcloud c3-16 is a compute-optimized virtual server powered by an AMD EPYC processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 8 dedicated physical cores with 8 vCPUs, operating at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz with no hyperthreading, ensuring one thread per core. The system is provisioned with 16.0 GB of RAM, yielding a 2.0 GB per core allocation. Local storage consists of a bundled 200 GB NVMe SSD, and network connectivity is anchored by a 1 Gbps baseline bandwidth with one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 25% of tested hardware. Multi-core operations, such as stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, perform in the average tier. Memory bandwidth tests, including read-only and write operations on large blocks, also align with average tier expectations. Text processing and text generation for small language models show strong performance, while medium model inference and database operations fall into the average category.

The c3-16 server offers a high-density compute profile with dedicated physical cores, which eliminates noisy-neighbor issues common in shared-thread environments. However, the 1:2 RAM-to-vCPU ratio limits its suitability for memory-intensive workloads. The inclusion of local NVMe storage provides fast read/write capabilities, making it highly efficient for CPU-bound tasks that require moderate local caching. This configuration is well-suited for software compilation, web serving, text processing, and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: The OVHcloud c3-16 is an x86_64 compute-optimized virtual server featuring 8 dedicated AMD EPYC physical cores running at 2.3 GHz with no hyperthreading. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and includes a bundled 200 GB NVMe SSD for local storage. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, the instance delivers 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks highlight strong single-core CPU execution and text processing capabilities, alongside average multi-core and memory bandwidth results. The dedicated core allocation ensures consistent compute performance, though the 1:2 RAM-to-vCPU ratio makes it less suitable for memory-heavy applications. This server is optimized for CPU-bound workloads such as software compilation, web serving, and lightweight text-generation inference.
og_description: OVHcloud c3-16 is a compute-optimized server featuring 8 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 16 GB RAM, and a 200 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core performance for CPU-bound workloads.
meta_description: OVHcloud c3-16 is a compute-optimized server with 8 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 16 GB RAM, and a 200 GB NVMe SSD on a KVM hypervisor.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores and bundled NVMe storage for consistent single-core performance.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated AMD EPYC physical cores with no hyperthreading
16.0 GB RAM providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio
Bundled 200 GB NVMe SSD local storage
Strong single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM virtualization
Designed for software compilation, web serving, and lightweight inference
